title: Detecting Odd Cycles in a Graph

statement.markdown: Implement a function to detect if a graph contains an odd cycle using DFS/BFS. Your function should return True if a cycle exists and False otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A graph represented as an adjacency list: {node: [neighbors]}.
statement.outputFormat: True if a cycle exists, False otherwise.
statement.notes: The graph may contain multiple nodes and edges.

examples.explanation: The graph contains an odd cycle: 1->2->1
No cycle exists in the graph

hints.text: Use a depth-first search (DFS) approach to traverse the graph

editorial.title: Detecting Odd Cycles in a Graph
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to use a recursive DFS function to traverse the graph. When a node is visited, check if it has already been visited before in the current DFS path. If it has, a cycle exists. We also need to keep track of the current DFS path to detect cycles.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to perform a DFS traversal of the graph and check for cycles at each step. However, this approach has a time complexity of O(n^2) because we need to check all nodes for cycles.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient approach is to use a recursive DFS function with a stack to keep track of the current DFS path. We can also use a boolean array to mark nodes that have been visited.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a recursive DFS function and a boolean array to detect cycles. We also need to check for back edges in the graph, which indicates the presence of a cycle.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can prove the correctness of the solution by showing that the DFS traversal visits all nodes in the graph and detects cycles correctly.
editorial.dryRun: Suppose we have a graph {1:[2,3],2:[1,4],3:[1,4],4:[]} and we want to detect if it contains an odd cycle using DFS.
commonMistakes: Failing to check for back edges in the graph
Using a brute force approach to detect cycles

functionSignature.java: public boolean detectCycle(int n, List<List<Integer>> adj);
functionSignature.python: def detectCycle(self, n, adj: list[list[int]]) -> bool:
